title: QUIC RFC 9000 Reordering And Gap Immediate ACK Scheduling Verification
verification_method: Requirement-home execution, deterministic fuzz/property sampling, reciprocal trace inspection, and SpecTrace render/check for the bounded reordering/gap immediate-ACK scheduling proof.
scope: This verification covers the supported ACK-generation consequence for processed Application Data packet numbers: lower ack-eliciting packets and higher ack-eliciting packets with missing packet numbers require immediate ACK scheduling, while contiguous ack-eliciting packets and non-ack-eliciting gap observations do not use this immediate-ACK trigger.
expected_result: Focused tests prove both positive immediate-ACK branches, the contiguous negative case, the non-ack-eliciting edge case, and deterministic fuzz/property classification across duplicate, lower, contiguous, and gapped second-packet observations. No parser, serializer, wire-format, hot-path production, loss-recovery, or congestion-control behavior is introduced by this proof slice.
procedure: Run the focused RFC 9000 requirement-home tests for S13P2P1-0013.
Render and check the touched SpecTrace artifacts.
Confirm the focused artifact set is reciprocal only for the intended S13P2P1 reordering/gap immediate-ACK requirement.
Confirm that no packet-number decoding, ACK frame serialization, loss recovery, congestion response, public API, or interop claim is introduced.
preconditions: Packet processing has already recovered packet numbers for the Application Data packet number space.
Packet processing has already classified whether each processed packet is ack-eliciting.
Peer loss detection and sender recovery are outside this receiver ACK scheduling proof slice.

supplemental_sections.heading: No Production Hot Path Change
supplemental_sections.content: This slice adds focused trace and requirement-home proof for existing ACK-generation behavior. It does not alter parser, serializer, wire-format, state-machine, or hot-path production code; deterministic fuzz/property coverage exercises the state classification without requiring a benchmark refresh.

status_summary: Passed locally on 2026-04-25. Focused reordering/gap immediate-ACK requirement-home tests passed, including deterministic fuzz/property coverage, and the focused reciprocal trace check linked only S13P2P1-0013 to the new artifact set.
